subject: Geology
title: Uranium and Nuclear Fuel
description: A 60-second lesson on Uranium and Nuclear Fuel.

hook.text: A small pellet of uranium produces as much energy as a ton of coal. The fuel comes from specific geologic deposits.

buildup.text: Uranium ore forms in sandstone, unconformity, and vein deposits. It must be mined, milled, and enriched for use.

discovery.text: Australia, Kazakhstan, and Canada hold the largest uranium reserves. Geology concentrates it in certain rock types.

twist.text: Spent nuclear fuel remains radioactive for thousands of years. Geologic disposal deep underground is the safest plan.

climax.text: Nuclear energy depends on geology from start to finish. The ground provides the fuel and must store the waste.

punchline.text: The ground gives the fuel and takes the waste.

quiz.question: Why is geologic disposal planned for nuclear waste?
options: Deep stable rock can isolate radioactive material for thousands of years
